heading: Comptroller of the Currency; appointment; term
label: 2 Comptroller of the Currency; appointment; term
text: The Comptroller of the Currency shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, and shall hold his office for a term of five years unless sooner removed by the President, upon reasons to be communicated by him to the Senate.
notes: CodificationR.S. § 325 derived from , , which was the National Bank Act. See .act June 3, 1864, ch. 106, § 113 Stat. 99section 38 of this titleProvisions of this section which prescribed the annual basic compensation of the Comptroller of the Currency were omitted to conform to the provisions of the Executive Schedule. See , Government Organization and Employees.section 5314 of Title 5

Amendments1935—Act , struck out “on the recommendation of the Secretary of the Treasury” after “President”, where first appearing, and changed the salary from “$5,000 a year” to “$15,000 a year”.Aug. 23, 1935
Repeals, , formerly cited as a credit to this section, was repealed by , , .Act Oct. 15, 1949, ch. 695, § 463 Stat. 880Pub. L. 89–554, § 8(a)80 Stat. 655Sept. 6, 1966

Exception as to Transfer of FunctionsFunctions vested by any provision of law in Comptroller of the Currency, referred to in this section, were not included in transfer of functions to Secretary of the Treasury, see note set out under .section 1 of this title
